See narrative under EU CR4. The institution applies the Standardised Approach and assigns risk weights in accordance with the CRR.

AS Inbank applies the Original Exposure Method for the calculation of derivative exposures under counterparty credit risk (CCR). Derivative exposures primarily relate to interest rate swaps and foreign exchange derivatives used for hedging purposes.
See narrative under EU CCR1. Counterparty credit risk exposures relate primarily to derivative transactions calculated under the Original Exposure Method.

In December 2024, AS Inbank executed a synthetic securitisation transaction referencing a portfolio of Polish retail green financing exposures. The transaction achieved Significant Risk Transfer under the CRR framework. The institution acts solely as originator. The securitisation remains in place during 2025 and continues to reduce risk-weighted exposure amounts.

The securitisation exposures are treated under the Standardised Approach in accordance with the CRR. The transaction relates to synthetic retail exposures where capital requirements are calculated under the SEC-SA approach.
The securitised portfolio relates to retail exposures originated by AS Inbank. Specific credit risk adjustments are recognised in accordance with accounting standards.

As at 31 December 2025, AS Inbank’s total own funds amounted to EUR 197 million (31 December 2024: EUR 179 million). The Total Capital ratio stood at 18.81% (31 December 2024: 18.90%).During 2024, the Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1), Tier 1 and Total Capital increased due to capital issuances and earned profit. In April 2024, AS Inbank raised EUR 2.34 million of additional Tier 1 capital. In August 2024, the institution raised EUR 10 million of additional capital from shareholders. In December 2024, AS Inbank executed a synthetic securitisation transaction qualifying for Significant Risk Transfer (SRT), which reduced risk-weighted exposure amounts (RWEA). As a result of capital raises, retained earnings and the securitisation transaction, capital ratios improved compared to 31 December 2023.In January 2025, AS Inbank called EUR 3.15 million of Tier 1 instruments and EUR 8 million of Tier 2 instruments, which resulted in a temporary reduction in own funds during the first half of 2025. In the second half of 2025, the institution strengthened its capital base by issuing EUR 8 million of Tier 2 capital in October 2025 and EUR 5 million of new CET1 capital in December 2025.The Total Risk Exposure Amount (RWEA) amounted to EUR 1,045 million as at 31 December 2025 (31 December 2024: EUR 947 million). The increase during 2025 was primarily driven by growth in retail lending exposures, partly offset by the continued effect of the synthetic securitisation transaction executed in 2024. The leverage ratio stood at 10.71% (31 December 2024: 10.53%), well above the regulatory minimum requirement of 3%. The increase reflects strengthened Tier 1 capital, partially offset by growth in total exposures.As at 31 December 2025, the Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) stood at 888% (31 December 2024: 524%), remaining significantly above the regulatory minimum requirement of 100%. The Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) stood at 135.74% (31 December 2024: 120.14%), supported by a stable retail deposit base and balanced funding structure.
As at 31 December 2025, the own funds requirement for CVA risk amounted to EUR 113,951, which is immaterial relative to the institution’s total capital requirements.

As at 31 December 2025, the Total Risk Exposure Amount (RWEA) amounted to EUR 1,045 million (31 December 2024: EUR 947 million). The increase during 2025 was primarily driven by growth in retail lending exposures. The RWEA level continues to reflect the synthetic securitisation transaction executed in December 2024, which achieved Significant Risk Transfer and reduced capital requirements for the referenced portfolio.
The scope of accounting consolidation and the scope of regulatory consolidation are the same.
Operational risk capital requirements are calculated using the Standardised Approach in accordance with the CRR.
Operational risk own funds requirements are derived from the Business Indicator and are included in total risk exposure amounts.
Not applicable. The accounting and regulatory scopes of consolidation are identical.

As at 31 December 2025, AS Inbank’s total own funds amounted to EUR 197 million and the Total Capital ratio stood at 18.81% (31 December 2024: 18.90%). The development of own funds during the year reflects capital issuances, instrument calls and retained earnings as described in the Key Metrics section.
There are no material differences between accounting carrying values and regulatory exposure amounts beyond standard prudential adjustments.
Due to the changes in Latvian and Polish countercyclical buffers, Inbank has a countercyclical buffer requirement of 1.16% of total risk exposure.

In 2025, there were two changes in countercyclical buffer (CCyB) requirements. In Estonia, Lithuania and Czechia, the buffer remained at the same level as it was on 31.12.2024. In Latvia, from 18.06.2025, the CCyB increased from 0.5% to 1.0%. In Poland, from 25.09.2025, the CCyB increased from 0.0% to 1.0%.

As at 31 December 2025, the Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) stood at 135.74% (31 December 2024: 120.14%), remaining well above the regulatory minimum requirement of 100%. The NSFR is supported by a stable retail deposit base and balanced asset-liability structure.

The Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR), disclosed as a 12-month trailing average, stood at 888% as at 31 December 2025. During 2025, movements in the LCR were primarily driven by changes in the level of high-quality liquid assets (HQLA), while net cash outflows remained relatively stable due to a predominantly retail funding base. The liquidity buffer mainly consists of central bank reserves and high-quality sovereign exposures.

The total exposure measure under the leverage ratio framework amounted to EUR 1,546 million as at 31 December 2025 (31 December 2024: EUR 1,403 million). The increase during the year was mainly driven by growth in loans and advances. Adjustments include the deduction of intangible assets and the inclusion of derivative exposures calculated under the Original Exposure Method.

AS Inbank applies the Standardised Approach for credit risk. Risk weights are assigned in accordance with the CRR. For exposures to institutions and central governments where applicable, external credit assessments from recognised ECAIs are used. The portfolio is predominantly composed of retail exposures.

AS Inbank applies the Standardised Approach for credit risk. Credit risk mitigation techniques primarily include collateral and guarantees where applicable. The majority of the retail portfolio consists of unsecured exposures. The institution does not apply advanced credit risk mitigation techniques or internal models.

he Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital, Tier 1 capital and Total Capital have been increasing during 2024. In April 2024, Inbank raised 2.34 million EUR of new Tier 1 capital. In August 2024, Inbank raised 10 million EUR of additional capital from shareholders. In December 2024, Inbank rolled out synthetic securitisation reducing RWEA. Due to the new capital raises, earned profit and synthetic securitisation, the capital ratios have improved compared to 31.12.2023. As a result of called back 3.15 million euro Tier 1 and 8 million euro Tier 2 bonds in January 2025, Inbank's own funds decreased slightly in first half of 2025.In 30.06.2025, the LCR stood at 1162%. The LCR levels have been improving in last years and are well above the minimum regulatory requirement of 100%. The LCR is supported by Retail term deposits funding. In 30.06.2025, the NSFR was 135.30% compared to a minimum regulatory requirement of 100%. The ratio has been realtively stable during last years where both the Available Stable Funding and the Required Stable Funding have increased.
